sentence_reviews.text: Adversarial security evaluation probes evasion, extraction, prompt injection, tool abuse, data poisoning, privacy leakage, and supply-chain compromise.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF frames adversarial testing, risk measurement, monitoring, privacy, security, robustness, and supply-chain risk management as AI governance concerns.
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports the broad risk-management scope, but the exact enumerated list is a taxonomy synthesis rather than a single directly supported statement.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Adversarial robustness & security testing is to connect Adversarial examples & adversarial training, Robustness, distribution shift & OOD evaluation to downstream capabilities including Red teaming, abuse testing & threat modeling, Fault tolerance, security, energy & cost optimization, Cybersecurity, finance & operational risk AI.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports adversarial testing, red-teaming, robustness, security, and operational risk governance; Deep Learning covers adversarial examples/training as robustness techniques.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependency relationship is defensible across the cited sources, but downstream capability wording is atlas graph terminology.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Red teaming, abuse testing & threat modeling
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF discusses adversarial testing, red-teaming, and risk identification as part of AI risk management.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supported as a graph relationship, not as exact source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Fault tolerance, security, energy & cost optimization
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports security and resilience governance, but not the full bundled energy/cost optimization phrase.
sentence_reviews.notes: Security and fault-tolerance aspects are supported; energy and cost optimization are not directly established by the cited packet source.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Cybersecurity, finance & operational risk AI
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF treats AI risk management as relevant to security, operational risk, and domain governance contexts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Defensible as a broad application synthesis; not directly verified for all named domains.

sentence_reviews.text: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Adversarial examples & adversarial training, Robustness, distribution shift & OOD evaluation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF emphasizes ongoing monitoring and contextual risk management; Deep Learning covers adversarial examples/training and robustness concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: The incompleteness claim is plausible but needs a more specific source; dependency list is graph-derived.

sentence_reviews.text: Adversarial robustness & security testing measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Adversarial examples & adversarial training.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning covers adversarial examples/training; NIST AI RMF supports adversarial testing and governance of robustness/security risks.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is well supported, though phrased in atlas edge vocabulary.

sentence_reviews.text: Adversarial robustness & security testing measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Robustness, distribution shift & OOD evaluation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports robustness and validity evaluation, monitoring, and risk measurement across deployment contexts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is defensible, but the exact dependency wording is graph-derived.

sentence_reviews.text: Adversarial robustness & security testing measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Caches, APIs & tool interfaces.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports security and risk management for AI systems, but does not directly establish cache/API/tool-interface dependence.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible for modern deployed systems, but this packet lacks a specific tool-interface security source.

edge_reviews.notes: Adversarial examples/training are a core basis for adversarial robustness/security evaluation.
Robustness/OOD evaluation is a core part of adversarial security evaluation, though supported through synthesis.
Tool and interface abuse is plausible, but the cited source set is too broad to establish the dependency strength directly.
